title: Dissociable Deficits in the Decision-Making Cognition of Chronic Amphetamine Abusers, Opiate Abusers, Patients with Focal Damage to Prefrontal Cortex, and Tryptophan-Depleted Normal Volunteers Evidence for Monoaminergic Mechanisms
notes: Foundational CGT paper establishing explicit-probability color choice and betting behavior metrics.

title: Differential effects of insular and ventromedial prefrontal cortex lesions on risky decision-making
notes: Confirms CGT as explicit-risk paradigm with separated probability judgment and betting components.

title: The contributions of lesion laterality and lesion volume to decision-making impairment following frontal lobe damage
notes: Supports interpretation of quality-of-decision and risk-adjustment measures.

title: Parcellating the neuroanatomical basis of impaired decision-making in traumatic brain injury
notes: Uses CGT measures including early-bet tendency and risk adjustment in a non-learning context.

title: Decision-making deficits in patients diagnosed with disordered gambling using the Cambridge Gambling task: the effects of substance use disorder comorbidity
notes: Provides explicit CGT implementation details (bet set 5/25/50/75/95 and auto-select-on-timeout behavior).

title: A computational model of the Cambridge gambling task with applications to substance use disorders
notes: Supports explicit mapping of box ratios and betting-stage latent parameters.
